Very important rules, very powerful rules that either you can follow and find yourself seamlessly falling into rapport with another human being or finding it almost impossible or breaking rapport when you're already in it for whatever reason. So let me go through a couple of these rules right now and they differ Number one, they differ between men and women so for starters when a man is influencing a man in a situation of sales or whatever my negotiation when a man influences a man, they should never stand directly across from one another. If you're the salesperson and you're trying to talk to someone in a sales situation if the man is here you want to go and stand off at an angle you want to corner off. Men should stand at corners As soon as two men face off like this it becomes confrontational and you get into the situation where you break rapport. Now if it was a multiple-day training I'd have us doing this experientially. There's no time. So just trust me that when you two men are like this it's like they start to back off. That's number one. Man with a woman. A man influencing a woman then you stand face-to-face women want to see you all of you and you keep your hands above waist level. You want to keep your hands where they can see them. This is very base stuff that goes back to like you know our primitive system here. So it's a gut reaction women get and just so you know women guys just fly out women are ten times more perceptive than men at figuring out body language. Women read people like in terms of when a woman, women true when you look at a man you see him from his shoes to the top of his head right. Women see the whole man. They'll see men say oh yeah, you know the color of his shoes what is his nails. I'm like how the hell did I know that? It's unbelievable. A man doesn't see all that. We're not as perceptive. We're just a man we'll see a couple of base things. Yeah, they look the right not bad. They get a gut reaction. The women see the whole organism. So we want to keep your hands in a played sight, okay? Women versus women influencing women corners. Man influencing a woman so face apart and don't invade the space bubble. This is number two a huge crucial thing. Do not invade someone's space bubble. I call these people space invaders. They yeah, who knows when they get right in your face and want to talk to you and typically there's spitters as well and you want to take it your spit guard out. You're like oh nothing more repulsive than a space invader. Typically in most societies there are certain like you know in Asia they tend to be a bit closer if you notice. Now the people stand closer together, but in most parts of the world it's two and a half to three feet is the space bubble. When you break that space bubble people find it a bit repulsive. They want to break off. So don't invade the space bubble. Next this is a very important one. It's called wrapping your package. Now I mentioned this before in terms of setting yourself up to be perceived as an authority figure. How you dress makes a huge difference in how you're perceived. Men wearing a suit and a tie is the most business like the most senior type of dress. When you dress with a suit and tie you just tend to say wow this person is senior he has it all together. Thanks. He looks good. I mean honestly I buy something from him. He looks honest. He looks like he's got it together to tell you the truth. Whether he does or not I have no idea. Although I've watched his expressions in my senses that he really does. Just so you know I've gotten some very good body. His body language is discursive, by and large. We'll talk about his active listening is very powerful. He listens and I know he gets me. That's his way of making rapport back to me. He knows I get him. We will have a rapport go. He's in my eyesight here, but the point is and we're gonna get to that in a second about how you actively listen. People either sense that you're in the loop or you're out of the loop. You're on the ball not on the ball. But simply by dressing that way you create this positive perception as a man. Same thing with women. Certain types of dress because women right here with the blonde hair. Am I standing up right here? Yeah. I haven't seen her whole outfit, but she looks good. She looks very serious. So if someone shakes your hand firmly, you shake it firmly back. You don't want to get into oh yeah you can get go I'll show you. You don't want to go go who's gonna. That's not right. Okay, and speaking of that let's talk about this thing of who's gonna blink first. Talk about eye contact. Unless you make and I'm gonna spit out a number here 72%. That's the scientifically proven this has all been tested by the way in labs around the world. That unless you make eye contact at least 72% of the time people will not trust you. I remember my son he's now 17 he had a friend growing up as kid Lucas nice kid. But I never trusted this kid because he always looked away. It's interesting he was a kid he's always like hi mr. Belfort and look at the ground and I was like I don't try. He's a nicest kid but because he had an issue with body if he didn't know how to make eye contact people don't trust you.
